methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Anthrogen is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Ankit Singhal that has raised $4.5M to date. The valuation universe comprises 3 public comparables in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics.

Of three valuation methods examined, only Stage-Progression proved applicable. This method uses the $4.5M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage transitions in this sector, yielding an implied valuation of $11M. Peer-Median anchoring was not feasible due to insufficient narrow-set comparables: while 16 same-sector peers exist, none met the threshold of 3+ datapoints matching stage, adjacent stage, or valuation tier simultaneously. Public-Comp Anchoring could not be executed because the target's revenue is not disclosed, preventing application of the public median P/S of 7.8x.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $10M (low) to $13M (high), centered on $11M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single method and highlights the primary uncertainty: lack of revenue visibility and scarcity of stage-matched private comparables. Any valuation work should be revisited once operational metrics or additional peer data become available.
